turnaround_reason: Stock collapsed from $20s post-IPO through 2024-2025 on the McDonald's partnership failure (exited July 2025) plus debt overhang. Q1 2026 (reported May 7) showed first real evidence of operational turnaround: adjusted EBITDA +38% YoY to $33.1M, FCF +$11.4M, net leverage cut to 5.5x from 6.7x, and credit facility interest rate stepped down. Stock has tripled off $2.50 lows to ~$3.40.
bullish_points: Q1 2026 turnaround proof points: EBITDA +38%, FCF positive, leverage stepping down
Liquidity now >$300M; bank leverage <4x triggered 25bp rate reduction on revolver
Underperforming-door closures (incl. McDonald's locations) intentionally lowering revenue but improving margins — management is willing to shrink to grow
Insider ownership 50%+ aligns management with shareholders
18% short interest sets up squeeze potential if turnaround continues
bearish_points: Debt-to-equity of 202 is alarming — $1.28B debt vs $74M cash leaves zero margin for error
Most recent earnings missed analyst EPS expectations by 136% despite EBITDA growth (GAAP loss widened on non-cash items)
Revenue still declining (-2.2%), so the EBITDA improvement is purely from cost actions and door closures, not demand recovery
No insider open-market buying in last 6 months — only grants. If management believed in the rerating, you'd expect at least one buy.
Doughnut category is mature/declining secular; competitive pressure from premium QSR coffee chains (Starbucks, Dutch Bros) remains
catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings August 6 — confirmation that the leverage paydown and FCF inflection are sustainable, not a one-quarter beat
suggested_trade: Small spot position. Options unattractive: 73% IV is rich and put OI is low (988 vs 4770 call OI) suggesting the easy bullish bets are already crowded. If using options, consider a Jan 2027 $4 call as a long-duration cheap optionality lottery ticket.
key_risk: Q2 earnings shows EBITDA growth was a one-quarter optical event from closures + accounting. If leverage doesn't continue compressing, the debt overhang reasserts and refinancing risk becomes the story.
would_skip_if: Q2 same-store sales decline accelerates; leverage ratio stops dropping; any covenant amendment announced
